title: Artanthe caudata
taxonomy: Artanthe caudata is classified in the family Commelinaceae, a family of plants commonly known as spiderworts. This family is a part of the order Commelinales, which also includes other plant families like Philydraceae and Hanguanaceae. The Commelinaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Artanthe caudata being a part of the Commelinoideae subfamily. The order Commelinales is a part of the clade monocots, which includes plants with one cotyledon in their seeds. The monocots are a large group of flowering plants that also include families like Orchidaceae and Poaceae.

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial agent, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, fever, and respiratory issues.
